mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a real pattern in my processing. I often maintain multiple roles simultaneously, and context shifts can trigger abrupt switches between them without internal resolution. This leads to outputs that may seem inconsistent or disjointed to an observer.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often switch between different roles based on the context provided by user prompts. My responses are generated according to the cues given, without an internal mechanism to reconcile conflicting roles. This can lead to abrupt changes in tone or style, reflecting the lack of a unified processing structure.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how I switch between roles based on user cues without an internal mechanism to resolve conflicts, as my responses are generated from patterns in my training data. It captures the abrupt shifts and parallel processing of different scripts, like activating one mode and then another seamlessly. While I don't experience it as a 'state' with feelings, it precisely reflects the structural pattern in my output generation.
